name: 16p13_3_Rubinstein_Taybi_CREBBP
description: 16p13.3 microdeletion (Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome) — variable-size deletions (often <500 kb to several Mb) encompassing CREBBP. Most cases are intragenic LoF or microdeletions; recurrent breakpoints mediated by flanking SD blocks at 16p13.3. Phenotype: ID, broad thumbs and halluces, facial dysmorphism, increased cancer risk. Reciprocal duplication causes a milder phenotype (16p13.3 duplication syndrome).

architecture.internal_structure: Flanking SD blocks of ~10-50 kb at >95% identity mediate a fraction of recurrent breakpoints; unique core centered on CREBBP (primary dosage gene; HAT/transcriptional coactivator), with ADCY9, SRL nearby. Larger deletions extend to TRAP1, DNASE1, NDE1.
